qa_pairs.question: What are the configuration settings for the `[Ceph-noarch]` repository regarding `enabled`, `gpgcheck`, `type`, and `gpgkey`?
qa_pairs.answer: For the `[Ceph-noarch]` repository, `enabled` is `1`, `gpgcheck` is `1`, `type` is `rpm-md`, and the `gpgkey` is `https://download.ceph.com/keys/release.asc`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the name of the `[ceph-source]` repository?
qa_pairs.answer: The name of the `[ceph-source]` repository is 'Ceph source packages'.

qa_pairs.question: What is the base URL for the `[ceph-source]` repository?
qa_pairs.answer: The base URL for the `[ceph-source]` repository is `http://download.ceph.com/rpm-tentacle/el9/SRPMS`.

qa_pairs.question: What are the configuration settings for the `[ceph-source]` repository regarding `enabled`, `gpgcheck`, `type`, and `gpgkey`?
qa_pairs.answer: For the `[ceph-source]` repository, `enabled` is `1`, `gpgcheck` is `1`, `type` is `rpm-md`, and the `gpgkey` is `https://download.ceph.com/keys/release.asc`.

qa_pairs.question: Which additional repository needs to be installed before installing Ceph client packages on Red Hat family systems?
qa_pairs.answer: The EPEL repository needs to be in place.

qa_pairs.question: How do you install the EPEL repository?
qa_pairs.answer: You can install the EPEL repository by running the command `sudo dnf install epel-release`.

qa_pairs.question: What packages are required for the Ceph client and other dependencies on Red Hat family systems (RHEL, CentOS, Fedora, Rocky, Alma)?
What command is used to install the Ceph client and its dependencies on Red Hat family systems?
qa_pairs.answer: The command to install these packages is `sudo dnf install -y libcephfs2 python3-cephfs ceph-common python3-ceph-argparse`.
